ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported
e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2; Morning, At Bedtime

ElementDescription: Over the last 30 days, please enter the average number of hours per week you engaged in free time activities, events, and experiences while you were not using alcohol or drugs
Over the last 30 days, please enter the average number of hours per week you engaged in free time activities, events, and experiences while you were using alcohol or drugs
Over the last 30 days, please rate on average how much you enjoyed free time activities, events, and experiences while you were not using alcohol or drugs.
Notes: 1 = Unpleasant; 2 = Neither pleasant nor unpleasant; 3=  Mildly pleasant; 4 = Moderately pleasant; 5 = Very pleasant; 6 = Extremely pleasant

ElementDescription: Over the last 30 days, please rate on average how easy it was to find free time activities, events, and experiences that did not involve using alcohol or drugs.
Notes: 1 = Extremely difficult; 2 = Moderately difficult; 3 = Slightly difficult; 4 = Slightly easy;  5 = Moderately easy; 6 = Extremely easy
